svg.text: Parallel processing across 8 attention heads - all heads compute simultaneously

g.text: • Head 0: Low-frequency color patterns, overall hue matching
• Head 2: Texture matching (grass, foliage, sky patterns)
• Heads 4-7: Other diverse correspondence patterns learned during training
All 8 attention heads compute simultaneously in a single batched operation
Not Sequential: Heads do NOT operate one after another (would be 8× slower)
Parallel GPU Execution: PyTorch batches all heads together → single efficient matrix multiply
Independent Learning: Each head learns different correspondence patterns via separate learned weights
Diverse Attention: Head 0 might focus on color, Head 1 on edges, Head 2 on texture, etc.
where Q[h], K[h] ∈ ℝ^(64×64) for head h
Each query position i gets probability distribution over all keys
O(N² × d) where N=64 positions, d=512 features
Attention matrix: [B, 8, 64, 64] ≈ 32K floats per batch item
With B=10, 8 heads: ~2.5 MB for attention weights
8 heads in parallel ≈ same time as 1 head (on modern GPUs)

svg.text: This cross-attention mechanism enables the network to find corresponding regions between IR and reference images despite different viewpoints
